calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Set For Trial On Sep-21-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Mar-18-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. REBECCA QUINN of Law Offices of Sanford M. Cipinko, Tel No. (415) 693-9905 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. BRYCE D. CARROLL of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendants The Hoff House, LLC, Keith Reiling, and Erin O'Donnell.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KEITH REILING'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT is overruled. First, the contract as alleged in the Complaint is not subject to the statute of frauds because, while unlikely, performance within one year cannot be deemed impossible. (See White Lighting Co. v. Wolfson (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343 fn. 2 ["there must not be the slightest possibility that it can be fully performed within one year"].) Further, whether performance in fact took longer than one year is irrelevant to determine whether the statute of fraud apples where, as here, the alleged terms of the contract do not indicate any such intent. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675 fn. 18 ["The applicability of the statute of frauds must be analyzed prospectively, based on the intentions of the parties and the terms of the agreement at the time it is made."].) Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the elements for each cause of action. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 [To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged.].) Third, the Complaint clearly alleges that Plaintiffs entered into oral agreements with both Defendants, and allegations as to emails confirming those agreements is irrelevant and does not render the Complaint uncertain. Next, Defendants have cited no authority establishing that non-compliance with Business & Professions Code sections 7159.5 or 7164 render the alleged contracts unenforceable. Additionally, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary duty based on the parties' relationship as partners and/or joint venturers. Further, the Complaint coherently pleads a breach of that duty. (See Compl. 67.) The Complaint also sufficiently alleges a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and is not a mere restatement of the claim for breach of contract. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to constructive fraud, accounting, and declaratory relief because Defendants' arguments lack merit. The Complaint plainly alleges facts sufficient to meet the heightened requirements for fraud claims (see Compl. 16, 20, 25, 27, 28, 78, 79), and each element of an accounting. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. BRYCE D. CARROLL of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendants The Hoff House, LLC, Keith Reiling, and Erin O'Donnell.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE HOFF HOUSE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT is overruled. First, the contract as alleged in the Complaint is not subject to the statute of frauds because, while unlikely, performance within one year cannot be deemed impossible. (See White Lighting Co. v. Wolfson (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343 fn. 2 ["there must not be the slightest possibility that it can be fully performed within one year"].) Further, whether performance in fact took longer than one year is irrelevant to determine whether the statute of fraud apples where, as here, the alleged terms of the contract do not indicate any such intent. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675 fn. 18 ["The applicability of the statute of frauds must be analyzed prospectively, based on the intentions of the parties and the terms of the agreement at the time it is made."].) Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the elements for each cause of action. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 [To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged.].) Third, the Complaint clearly alleges that Plaintiffs entered into oral agreements with both Defendants, and allegations as to emails confirming those agreements is irrelevant and does not render the Complaint uncertain. Next, Defendants have cited no authority establishing that non-compliance with Business & Professions Code sections 7159.5 or 7164 render the alleged contracts unenforceable. Additionally, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary duty based on the parties' relationship as partners and/or joint venturers. Further, the Complaint coherently pleads a breach of that duty. (See Compl. 67.) The Complaint also sufficiently alleges a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and is not a mere restatement of the claim for breach of contract. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to constructive fraud, accounting, and declaratory relief because Defendants' arguments lack merit. The Complaint plainly alleges facts sufficient to meet the heightened requirements for fraud claims (see Compl. 16, 20, 25, 27, 28, 78, 79), and each element of an accounting. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. REBECCA QUINN of Law Offices of Sanford M. Cipinko, Tel No. (415) 693-9905 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. BRYCE D. CARROLL of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendants The Hoff House, LLC, Keith Reiling, and Erin O'Donnell.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ERIN O DONNELL'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT is overruled. First, the contract as alleged in the Complaint is not subject to the statute of frauds because, while unlikely, performance within one year cannot be deemed impossible. (See White Lighting Co. v. Wolfson (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343 fn. 2 ["there must not be the slightest possibility that it can be fully performed within one year"].) Further, whether performance in fact took longer than one year is irrelevant to determine whether the statute of fraud apples where, as here, the alleged terms of the contract do not indicate any such intent. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675 fn. 18 ["The applicability of the statute of frauds must be analyzed prospectively, based on the intentions of the parties and the terms of the agreement at the time it is made."].) Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the elements for each cause of action. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 [To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged.].) Third, the Complaint clearly alleges that Plaintiffs entered into oral agreements with both Defendants, and allegations as to emails confirming those agreements is irrelevant and does not render the Complaint uncertain. Next, Defendants have cited no authority establishing that non-compliance with Business & Professions Code sections 7159.5 or 7164 render the alleged contracts unenforceable. Additionally, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary duty based on the parties' relationship as partners and/or joint venturers. Further, the Complaint coherently pleads a breach of that duty. (See Compl. 67.) The Complaint also sufficiently alleges a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and is not a mere restatement of the claim for breach of contract. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to constructive fraud, accounting, and declaratory relief because Defendants' arguments lack merit. The Complaint plainly alleges facts sufficient to meet the heightened requirements for fraud claims (see Compl. 16, 20, 25, 27, 28, 78, 79), and each element of an accounting. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
